claims.quote: The first of its kind in the U.S. and newly launched, the UCLA Space Medicine Fellowship aims to develop the next generation of flight surgeons who will support the health, safety, and well-being of human space flight and planetary expeditions.
claims.claimText: UCLA Space Medicine Fellowship — newly launched program training flight surgeons.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship program info — post-residency trainee track, Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family FELLOWSHIP_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: The two-year program will include rotations at SpaceX and a specialized engineering curriculum with the California Institute of Technology (Caltech) and NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), which is managed by Caltech.
claims.claimText: Two-year fellowship with rotations at SpaceX and curriculum with Caltech and NASA JPL.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship duration and partner sites — trainee Tier 2.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family FELLOWSHIP_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: The two-year program begins in July 2022, with its inaugural fellow Dr. Haig Aintablian, who recently completed his emergency medicine residency at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA.
claims.claimText: Program began July 2022 with inaugural fellow from DGSOM EM residency.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship start-date / inaugural fellow — Tier 2 trainee track.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family FELLOWSHIP_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: Applications for subsequent years will begin in November 2022 and be open to graduates of four-year ACGME/OGME accredited Emergency Medicine residencies who are eligible for a California medical license.
claims.claimText: Eligibility: graduates of 4-yr ACGME/OGME accredited EM residencies eligible for CA medical license.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship eligibility requirements — Tier 2 trainee track.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family FELLOWSHIP_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: “We are excited to be joining forces with Caltech, JPL and SpaceX to prepare future flight surgeons to deal with the unique medical challenges posed for people in space and the effects of space travel on the human body in this quickly growing field,” said Dr. Jo Feldman, director of the fellowship.
claims.claimText: Dr. Jo Feldman is director of the fellowship.
claims.tierAssignmentRationale: Fellowship director — Tier 2 trainee program leadership.
claims.visibilityRationale: source family FELLOWSHIP_PAGE is future-lane only

claims.quote: Under UCLA, fellows will train in biomechanical engineering with the UCLA Samueli School of Engineering, undergo austere medical training in a polar climate, participate in Mars analog missions in Utah and conduct research with the Exploration Medical Capability Element of NASA’s Human Research Program.

claims.quote: “With deep space travel on the not-so-distant horizon, UCLA’s space medicine program is timely.
